landing.subtitle: Understand which regulations, licences, and compliance obligations apply to your startup — globally.

features.wizardDesc: Answer 4 simple questions. Get a full compliance roadmap for your activity and jurisdiction.
features.searchDesc: Ask any compliance question in plain language. Get instant, contextual answers powered by Claude.
features.compareDesc: Stack multiple activities in one jurisdiction. See your consolidated obligations at a glance.

landing.stepperSubtitle: Three modes depending on what you need. Mix and match to fit your situation.

check.title: Check
check.desc: Tell us about your activities and jurisdictions. Get a complete regulatory report with regimes, licences, obligations, timeline and cost.

learn.title: Learn
learn.desc: Dive into curated paths, visual maps, and the XRPL deep-dive. Understand MiCA, Howey, custody classification and more.

ask.title: Ask AI
ask.desc: Any precise question? Ask in plain English or French. Instant answer from the knowledge base or from Claude.

landing.xrplDesc: Native support for XRP, AMM (XLS-30), NFT (XLS-20), MPT (XLS-33), RLUSD, Payment Channels, Escrow, and 10 custody implementations.

step1.title: What does your project do?
step1.subtitle: Select the activity that best describes your project.

report.title: Compliance Report
report.aiSubtitle: Personalised roadmap, risk analysis and recommendations — auto-generated

examples.1: Do I need a CASP licence for my XRPL DEX front-end in France?
examples.2: What's the difference between EMT and ART under MiCA?
examples.3: How does MPT custody work on XRPL?
examples.4: What is the Liechtenstein TVTG framework?

licence.question: What authorization must you obtain?
licence.examples: Examples: CASP, MSB, MTL, BitLicense, VASP, EMI, PSAN, DASP

regime.question: What is the law or framework itself?
regime.examples: Examples: MiCA, BSA, TVTG, GENIUS Act, CLARITY Act, MiFID II, PSD2

obligation.question: What must you do on a daily basis to comply?
obligation.examples: Examples: KYC, KYB, AML, CFT, Travel Rule, SAR

token.question: What is your asset classified as?
token.examples: Examples: EMT, ART, STO, ICO, NFT, RWA, RLUSD, MPT, DPT

regulator.question: Who supervises you?
regulator.examples: Examples: AMF, SEC, CFTC, FinCEN, FATF, FINMA, MAS, SFC, FCA, VARA

infra.question: Technical layers and ecosystem concepts
infra.examples: Examples: TradFi, CeFi, DeFi, DAO, Smart Contract, DLT, Trust Line, IOU, Escrow, SignerList

local.label: Local
local.tooltip: Applies only if you physically operate there
local.explanation: Territorial — applies only if you incorporate or operate physically in that jurisdiction. Example: BitLicense applies only to businesses operating in New York.

extra.label: Extraterritorial
extra.tooltip: Applies when you serve users from this jurisdiction, even if you're based elsewhere
extra.explanation: Extraterritorial — applies whenever you serve or target users of that jurisdiction, regardless of where your company is incorporated. Example: MiCA applies to any business serving EU users, even if based outside the EU.

global.label: Global standard
global.tooltip: International standard adopted across most jurisdictions
global.explanation: International standard — adopted or transposed by most jurisdictions worldwide. Example: FATF's Travel Rule and KYC/AML principles are applied almost universally.

legend.title: What do the flags, topics and scopes mean?
legend.flagsBody: Indicate where the regulation, regulator or licence comes from. 🌐 = international body or rule adopted globally.
legend.topicsBody: Cross-cutting classification of what each term is — a licence, a regime, a compliance duty, etc.
legend.scopesBody: Tells you WHEN a regulation applies to you — based on where you operate, where your users are, or as a global standard.

xrpl.title: XRPL Regulatory Hub
xrpl.subtitle: Everything you need to know about XRPL compliance — XRP legal status, native features, and custody implementations.

common.disclaimer: Regul8 provides general information for educational purposes only. This is not legal advice. Information may not be up to date. Always consult a qualified lawyer specializing in digital asset law for advice specific to your situation.
